source_metadata.title_raw: Dr. Francisco Reveles - I-18-045 - March 30, 2018 - Browns Valley

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 30, 2018

Dr. Francisco Reveles
Yuba County Superintendent of Schools
P.O. Box 594
Browns Valley, CA 95918

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-18-045

Dear Mr. Reveles:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the statement of economic interest provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act").¹ Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71); any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

QUESTION

Does the Act require you to amend your Statement of Economic Interest to disclose your real estate and source of income interests enumerated below?

CONCLUSION

No. According to your local agency's conflict of interest code, you do not have to disclose the interests.

FACTS AS PROVIDED BY REQUESTOR

You are the Yuba County Superintendent of Schools. In attempting to complete your Statement of Economic Interest (Form 700), you referenced the adopted Yuba County Office of Education Bylaws pertaining to designated persons and disclosure categories. Your potentially disclosable interests are as follows:

• A rental property located in another county that was sold in July 2017.
• Income from honoraria for motivational speeches in the counties of Santa Clara, Sacramento, and San Joaquin. All speeches and sponsoring agencies were located outside your

________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-045
Page No. 2

voting and jurisdictional areas and do not have an interest, impact, or influence on your
performance of your duties as County Superintendent.
• Income from a university course you taught in Sacramento County, again with no direct or
indirect connection to your duties as County Superintendent.

Your agency's conflict of interest code requires the disclosure of the following for an
individual serving as a "Superintendent of Schools":

(a) Interests in real property located entirely or partly within county boundaries, or within
two miles of county boundaries, or of any land owned or used by the County Office of Education.
Such interests include any leasehold, beneficial, or ownership interest or option to acquire such
interest in real property.

(b) Investments or business positions in or income from sources which:

"(1) are engaged in the acquisition or disposal of real property within the county;
"(2) are contractors or subcontractors which are or have been within the past two
years engaged in work or services of the type used by the County Office of
Education, or;
"(3) manufacture or sell supplies, books, machinery, or equipment of the type used
by the County Office of Education."

Neither your real estate nor sources of income from motivational speeches and the
university course were in any way connected to any services used by the Yuba County Office of
Education. Per advice you were given, you only filed the cover page to the Form 700 and now
inquire whether you must amend the Form to include any applicable schedules.

ANALYSIS

The Act requires most state and local government officials and employees to publicly
disclose their personal assets and income. They also must disqualify themselves from participating
in decisions that may affect their personal economic interests. A "public official" is defined as
"every member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local government agency…" (Section
82048.) As County Superintendent, you are a public official.

Public officials are required to file Form 700 when their position is specified in Section
87200 or when the duties of the position include making or participating in the making of decisions
that may foreseeably have a material effect on any financial interest; and, as a result, their position
is designated in an agency's conflict of interest code. (Section 87302.) The Act early designates that the
applicable local agency serve as the "code reviewing body." The code reviewing body is charged
with reviewing and approving an agency's conflict of interest code. (See Section 87309.) Once
approved, your agency's conflict of interest code becomes legally effective and mandates public
officials to complete and file Form 700.

Form 700 instructs officials that "if [they] discover errors or omissions on any statement,
[they must] file an amendment as soon as possible. [They] are only required to amend the schedule
that needs to be revised; it is not necessary to refile the entire form." In this case, your only

File No. A-18-045
Page No. 3

potentially disclosable real estate interest is located outside of two miles from the jurisdiction² and
the only potentially disclosable sources of income do not meet any of the reportable criteria
established by your local agency. Based on the facts provided, you do not have to amend the Form
700.³

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Brian G. Lau
Assistant General Counsel

By: Ryan P. O'Connor
Counsel, Legal Division

RPOC:jgl

² Please note that "jurisdiction" and "county offices" are defined terms on the Form 700 Pamphlet.

³ Note that we are providing only technical assistance regarding compliance with your reporting requirements
under your agency's adopted conflict of interest code. (Regulation 18329.5(b).) We express no opinion regarding the
adequacy of the adopted conflict of interest code. Any questions regarding the adequacy of the adopted code, must first
be submitted to the agency's code reviewing body. (Regulation 18329(a).)
